title: Flaky Uruguayan Lentil Empanadas
description: A fermented, flaky empanada featuring a savory lentil filling seasoned with Italian herbs, perfect for a cozy solo dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, salt, and yeast.
Add warm water and mix to form a rough dough; knead for 5–7 minutes until smooth.
Stir in the olive oil until fully incorporated, then cover the dough and let it rise at room temperature for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
While the dough is rising, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and sauté onion and garlic until translucent.
Add lentils, Italian seasoning, smoked paprika, and tomato paste; cook for 2 minutes, then remove from heat.
Stir in the grated cheese and season with freshly ground black pepper to taste, letting the mixture cool slightly.
Preheat the oven to 200 °C (392 °F). Punch down the dough, divide it into 8 equal portions, and roll each into a 10 cm circle.
Place a spoonful of filling on the center of each circle, fold over to create a half‑moon shape, and crimp the edges to seal.
Arrange the empanadas on a parchment‑lined baking sheet, brush the tops with beaten egg or a little olive oil, and bake for 20–25 minutes until golden and flaky.
Serve the empanadas warm, ideally with a fresh green salad or a simple vinaigrette.
